_doc: Singles teambuilding archetype model (T-101). Every team has ONE base archetype (balanced|offensive|defensive) plus a weighted, STACKABLE gimmick layer (Batch-3 owner decision). The 2C engine (T-107) reads `entry` to detect emergent crystallization from a partial team, and `structure` as the soft preferred composition to complete/refine toward (never fixed slots). `feature`/`role` keys are team-features the engine detects on candidate mons (see featureDefinitions). Values encode the owner-validated research (docs/research/team-archetypes.md + rating-decisions.md) and are PROVISIONAL per each task's meta-analysis validation clause.

featureDefinitions.regeneratorPivot: bulky mon with Regenerator (or a bulky pivot with recovery) that switches to reset damage
featureDefinitions.hazardSetter: sets Stealth Rock / Spikes / Toxic Spikes
featureDefinitions.wallbreaker: high-power (often Choice) attacker that breaks walls
featureDefinitions.winCondition: a setup sweeper or late-game closer (Z-move / boosting sweep)
featureDefinitions.setupSweeper: boosts its own stats then sweeps (DD / SD / CM / QD / Shift Gear / Unburden)
featureDefinitions.priorityUser: carries a priority move as revenge / finisher
featureDefinitions.unawareWall: Unaware (or Magic Bounce) wall that denies setup / hazards
featureDefinitions.choiceScarfRevengeKiller: Choice Scarf mon used to revenge-kill / provide speed control
featureDefinitions.pivotUser: U-turn / Volt Switch / Flip Turn / Teleport pivot
featureDefinitions.focusSashLead: frail Focus Sash suicide lead (hazards + Taunt)
featureDefinitions.weatherSetter: sets weather via ability (Drizzle / Drought / Sand Stream / Snow Warning) or a weather move
featureDefinitions.weatherAbuser: Swift Swim / Chlorophyll / Sand Rush / Slush Rush, or a weather-boosted attacker
featureDefinitions.trickRoomAbuser: slow, high-offense mon that benefits from Trick Room

baseArchetypes.name: Balance
baseArchetypes.description: Defensive pivot core (Regenerator) that soaks hits and generates momentum for 1-2 Choice wallbreakers, wins the hazard game, and closes with a setup/Z win condition.

baseArchetypes.name: Bulky Offense
baseArchetypes.description: Offensively slanted with a small defensive backbone; wallbreakers + a Choice-Scarf revenge killer apply pressure (optionally a Pursuit trapper opens the breaker's path), closing with setup/priority.

baseArchetypes.name: Hyper Offense
baseArchetypes.description: Maximum aggression with little defense: a Focus-Sash suicide lead sets hazards and Taunts, then a stack of setup sweepers race to win, backed by priority / VoltTurn.

baseArchetypes.name: Full Stall
baseArchetypes.description: Passive defensive wall-stack: splits physical/special defense, denies win conditions (Unaware / Magic Bounce), and grinds foes with residual damage + Wish/Recover/Regenerator + clerics.

gimmicks.name: Weather (Rain/Sun/Sand/Snow)
gimmicks.description: A weather setter powers its own side (Swift Swim/Chlorophyll Speed, boosted attacks); glue keeps the plan alive after weather lapses, with a weather-independent backup win condition.

gimmicks.name: Screens
gimmicks.description: Dual screens / Aurora Veil blunt incoming hits so setup sweepers can boost safely behind them.

gimmicks.name: Trick Room
gimmicks.description: Inverts the speed order so slow, high-power abusers move first. Rare in singles OU but a valid gimmick.
